questions.stem: Unity feedback closed loop system with open loop TF G(s). Nyquist plot intersects real axis at Point A = -0.585 (ω = 10.2 rad/s) and unit circle at Point B = -0.97 - j0.21 (ω = 7.72 rad/s). Find the gain margin in dB.
common_errors: Inverting the formula: using 20log(|G(jωpc)|) instead of 20log(1/|G(jωpc)|)
Taking |G| at wrong frequency (using ω=7.72 instead of ω=10.2)
questions.solution_method: At phase crossover frequency ω=10.2 rad/s, the Nyquist plot crosses the real axis at -0.585, so |G(jωpc)| = 0.585. GM = 20log(1/0.585) = 4.65 dB.

questions.stem: Unity feedback closed loop system with open loop TF G(s). Nyquist plot intersects unit circle at Point B = -0.97 - j0.21 (ω = 7.72 rad/s). Find the phase margin in degrees.
common_errors: Using 180 - angle instead of 180 + angle (sign error when point is in third quadrant)
Computing angle of Point B incorrectly by ignoring negative real part
questions.solution_method: At gain crossover frequency ω=7.72 rad/s, G(jωgc) = -0.97 - j0.21. Angle = 180 + arctan(0.21/0.97) = 180 + 12.2 = 192.2° from positive real axis → PM = 180 - 167.8 = 12.2°. Equivalently PM = 180 + ∠G(jωgc) = 180 + (-167.8) = 12.2°.

questions.stem: Unity feedback closed loop system with open loop TF G(s). If the open loop gain is increased by a factor of 1.709, what will be the new phase crossover frequency?
common_errors: Assuming gain change shifts the phase crossover frequency
Confusing gain crossover frequency with phase crossover frequency
questions.solution_method: Gain variation does not affect the phase plot of G(jω). The phase crossover frequency is defined where ∠G(jω) = -180°, which is independent of gain magnitude. Therefore the phase crossover frequency remains 10.2 rad/s. (Alternatively: scaling by 1.709 moves the real-axis intersection from -0.585 to -0.585×1.709 ≈ -1, but the frequency at which the phase is -180° is unchanged.)
key_formulas: ω_pc is defined by ∠G(jω_pc) = -180°, independent of gain K

notes: Single question with three sub-parts, all on Nyquist-based GM/PM computation. Part C tests conceptual understanding that gain scaling does not affect phase crossover frequency.
